pr.title: Matched HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress by rewriting the loop and pulling allocations out of the loop
pr.author: DavidWeaverAudio

summary: PR 2571 matched HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress in src/sysdolphin/baselib/synth.c by reshaping the inner count-controlled readdress loop from an if-plus-do-while form into a for loop, hoisting count and j declarations to function scope, and changing one pointer increment expression to use uintptr_t arithmetic. The bot report records one new match for main/sysdolphin/baselib/synth, raising HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress from 96.56% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; contains HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress loop rewrite and local declaration changes.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: @@ -355,8 +355,10 @@ void HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress(AXVPB* arg0, void* callback)

what_changed.text: Added function-scope locals int count and int j in HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress.
Moved count assignment inside the outer while loop from a block-scoped declaration to an assignment to the hoisted local.
Replaced the guarded do-while inner loop with a for loop over j from 0 to count, preserving the same q-based field updates and q += 0x40 stride.
Changed p advancement from p += (count << 6) to p = (u8*) ((count << 6) + (uintptr_t) p), followed by the existing p += 0x10.
Automated report recorded one new match: HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress reached 100.00% in main/sysdolphin/baselib/synth.

smart_moves.text: Tried a more natural count-controlled for loop instead of preserving a generated-looking if-plus-do-while shape; this aligns with the loaded natural-loops standard and the bot report shows the function matched.
Kept q as the moving inner-loop pointer while p remains the outer group pointer advanced after the inner loop.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For MWCC matching, a count-controlled do-while guarded by if (count > 0) may need to be expressed as a for loop with an explicit index local when the source operation is naturally zero-or-more iterations.
Local declaration placement can be part of the match: this PR hoisted count and j out of the loop/function body region rather than declaring count inside the while loop.
Pointer arithmetic expression shape can matter for matching; here, p advancement used an explicit uintptr_t cast before casting back to u8*.

naming_conventions.text: No global or semantic symbol renames were made. The change used short locals already typical of low-level pointer loops: p, q, i, j, count, and delta.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: void HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress(AXVPB* arg0, void* callback)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Rewrite inner loop as for (j = 0; j < count; j++) rather than guarded do-while.
Hoist loop-related locals count and j to function scope to influence declaration/lifetime/register allocation shape.
Use explicit integer pointer addition through uintptr_t for p advancement.

review_feedback.text: decomp-dev bot reported matched code increased by 244 bytes overall and listed one new match: HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress in main/sysdolphin/baselib/synth reached 100.00%.
No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: count-controlled do while for loop MWCC match

evidence_refs: changed_files: one changed file, added 11 deleted 11
notes: Strong evidence for the exact code changes because the full raw diff is loaded and untruncated.
Strong evidence for the automated match result from the loaded bot report, but no local build, objdiff, or checkdiff logs beyond that report are present in this slice.
The PR body is empty and there are no human review comments, so author rationale beyond the title and diff should not be over-interpreted.
The PR title says 'pulling allocations out of the loop', but the diff evidence shows hoisting local declarations/loop variables, not heap allocation calls.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Count-controlled pointer-stride loops may match as explicit for loops
accepted_candidate_records.text: In HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress, replacing an if (count > 0) guarded do-while with for (j = 0; j < count; j++) over the same q += 0x40 stride was part of the PR that produced a 100.00% bot-reported match.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Function-scope loop locals affected this synth match
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR hoisted count and j declarations to function scope and assigned count inside the outer while loop; the loaded bot report then recorded HSD_SynthSFXGroupDataReaddress as matched.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize this PR into a rule that all guarded do-while loops should become for loops; the evidence is one function and one bot-reported match.
Do not treat the title phrase 'allocations' as proof of heap allocation behavior; the diff only shows local declaration placement changes.
Do not promote the uintptr_t pointer arithmetic shape as global style; it is supported here only as a narrow matching tactic.
